title: corydalis verticillaris subsp. boissieri
intro: Corydalis verticillaris subsp. boissieri, also known as the Boissier's corydalis, is a rare and endangered subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the Corydalis genus. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, which are attributed to its alkaloid content, particularly berberine. The medicinal properties of Corydalis verticillaris subsp. boissieri have been reported to provide relief from pain, reduce inflammation, and relax muscles, making it beneficial for treating conditions such as arthritis, fibromyalgia, and menstrual cramps. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of tinctures, infusions, and capsules, and can be combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
